abstract.text: At Apple, we believe in building products with both great features and great privacy. Learn how to apply privacy engineering techniques to your apps so you can build trust with users to unlock better experiences and engagement.  And find out how new privacy features can benefit you and your users.

inlineContent.text: Be the hero to change that by watching the video or reading the transcript on WWDCIndex and providing notes! It’s super easy:

abstract.text: Xcode 10, Swift 4.2, iOS 12, macOS 10.14 (Mojave), tvOS 12, watchOS 5.

doc://WWDCNotes/documentation/WWDCNotes/.title: Your Apps and the Future of macOS Security

abstract.text: Apple is on a mission to advance the state of Mac security, and we want your apps to be there with us. Learn about new protections for user data, new capabilities with Developer ID, and how you can best secure your apps.
Session notes shared by the community for the community.

HealthKit provides a consolidated repository for a variety of data including health records containing information such as lab results, immunizations, and medications downloaded directly from institutions. Discover new health records classes and types created to provide easy access to high-level record types without preventing access to the underlying raw data.  Learn new authorization techniques that allows your app to access health record data for analysis and presentation in a manner that respects your customer’s privacy.

Whether you use web content in your app via an embedded WebKit view, deliver pure web apps, or create web content to be embedded by others, it’s important to ensure that you are using the most current web security standards. Learn techniques like such as special cookie attributes and HTTP response headers designed to secure your web content against the latest threats that include cross-site scripting, cross-site request forgeries, and speculative execution attacks, commonly known as Spectre.

doc://WWDCNotes/documentation/WWDCNotes/.title: Handle interruptions and alerts in UI tests

abstract.text: Learn how to anticipate potential interruptions to your app’s interface and build smart tests to identify them. UI interruptions often appear indeterminately, typically during onboarding or first launch, which can make them hard to track down. Learn how to understand interruptions, write stronger tests with UI interruption handlers, and manage expected alerts.
